source: CIS GOOGLE KUBERNETES ENGINE (GKE) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF
title: Ensure Control Plane Authorized Networks is Enabled

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE KUBERNETES ENGINE (GKE) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF
input_row.title: Ensure Control Plane Authorized Networks is Enabled
input_row.description: Enable Control Plane Authorized Networks to restrict access to the cluster's control plane to only an allowlist of authorized IPs.
input_row.rationale: Authorized networks are a way of specifying a restricted range of IP addresses that are permitted to access your cluster's control plane. Kubernetes Engine uses both Transport Layer Security (TLS) and authentication to provide secure access to your cluster's control plane from the public internet. This provides you the flexibility to administer your cluster from anywhere; however, you might want to further restrict access to a set of IP addresses that you control. You can set this restriction by specifying an authorized network. Control Plane Authorized Networks blocks untrusted IP addresses. Google Cloud Platform IPs (such as traffic from Compute Engine VMs) can reach your master through HTTPS provided that they have the necessary Kubernetes credentials. Restricting access to an authorized network can provide additional security benefits for your container cluster, including: • Better protection from outsider attacks: Authorized networks provide an additional layer of security by limiting external, non-GCP access to a specific set of addresses you designate, such as those that originate from your premises. This helps protect access to your cluster in the case of a vulnerability in the cluster's authentication or authorization mechanism. • Better protection from insider attacks: Authorized networks help protect your cluster from accidental leaks of master certificates from your company's premises. Leaked certificates used from outside GCP and outside the authorized IP ranges (for example, from addresses outside your company) are still denied access. Impact: When implementing Control Plane Authorized Networks, be careful to ensure all desired networks are on the allowlist to prevent inadvertently blocking external access to your cluster's control plane.
input_row.audit: Using Google Cloud Console: 1. Go to Kubernetes Engine by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/kubernetes/list 2. From the list of clusters, click on the cluster to open the Details page and make sure 'Master authorized networks' is set to 'Enabled'. Using Command Line: To check Master Authorized Networks status for an existing cluster, run the following command; gcloud container clusters update $CLUSTER_NAME --zone $COMPUTE_ZONE --enable- master-authorized-networks The output should return { "enabled": true } if Control Plane Authorized Networks is enabled. If Master Authorized Networks is disabled, the above command will return null ({ }).
input_row.remediation: Using Google Cloud Console: 1. Go to Kubernetes Engine by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/kubernetes/list 2. Select Kubernetes clusters for which Control Plane Authorized Networks is disabled 3. Within the Details pane, under the Networking heading, click on the pencil icon named Edit control plane authorised networks. 4. Check the box next to Enable control plane authorised networks. 5. Click SAVE CHANGES. Using Command Line: To enable Control Plane Authorized Networks for an existing cluster, run the following command: gcloud container clusters update <cluster_name> --zone <compute_zone> -- enable-master-authorized-networks Along with this, you can list authorized networks using the --master-authorized- networks flag which contains a list of up to 20 external networks that are allowed to connect to your cluster's control plane through HTTPS. You provide these networks as a comma-separated list of addresses in CIDR notation (such as 90.90.100.0/24). Default Value: By default, Control Plane Authorized Networks is disabled. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/how-to/authorized-networks
input_row.impact: When implementing Control Plane Authorized Networks, be careful to ensure all desired networks are on the allowlist to prevent inadvertently blocking external access to your cluster's control plane.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control "Ensure Control Plane Authorized Networks is Enabled" can be automatically audited using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud). The audit process involves verifying a specific configuration property of GKE clusters that determines whether Control Plane Authorized Networks are enabled. This is a standard GCP service property related to network access control, and it can be queried directly via the available APIs and SDK.

The audit instructions provide a clear command-line method using the "gcloud" command to check if the "Master Authorized Networks" feature is enabled for a GKE cluster. This capability indicates that the control is API-checkable and aligns with criteria for automated verification.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_gke_cluster_master_authorized_networks_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **GCP API/SDK Method:** Use Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) to query the status of Master Authorized Networks for Kubernetes Engine clusters.
- **GCP CLI Command:** Execute `gcloud container clusters describe $CLUSTER_NAME --zone $COMPUTE_ZONE --format="get(masterAuthorizedNetworksConfig.enabled)"` to determine if `masterAuthorizedNetworksConfig.enabled` is set to true.
- **Resources to Enumerate:** Iterate over all GKE clusters within the account and region.
- **Configuration Property to Validate:** Check the `masterAuthorizedNetworksConfig.enabled` property.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - **Pass:** If `masterAuthorizedNetworksConfig.enabled` is `true`.
  - **Fail:** If `masterAuthorizedNetworksConfig.enabled` is `false` or not set.
- **Expected Values:** The configuration should return `true`, indicating that Control Plane Authorized Networks are enabled for added security.
step2_review:
---

### 1. APPROACH VALIDATION: **AGREE**

- **Manual vs Automated:** The classification as "Automated" is correct. This control can be fully validated using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud`). No manual review is required if all clusters are enumerated.
- **GCP API/SDK/CLI:** The property `masterAuthorizedNetworksConfig.enabled` is exposed via the GKE API and is accessible through `gcloud container clusters describe`. This is the canonical method for checking this setting.
- **Other Automation Options:** GCP Config (now called "Google Cloud Asset Inventory" and "Config Validator") can also be used to monitor this property, but the CLI/API approach is sufficient and standard.

---

### 2. TECHNICAL ACCURACY: **Score: 9/10**

- **Automation Details:** Technically correct. The command:
  ```
  gcloud container clusters describe $CLUSTER_NAME --zone $COMPUTE_ZONE --format="get(masterAuthorizedNetworksConfig.enabled)"
  ```
  will return `true` or `false` as expected.
- **Enumeration:** The assessment correctly notes the need to iterate over all clusters, but does not explicitly provide the enumeration command. For completeness, this should be:
  ```
  gcloud container clusters list --format="value(name,zone)"
  ```
  and then loop over each cluster.
- **Program Name:** The name `gcp_gke_cluster_master_authorized_networks_enabled` is clear and descriptive, but could be slightly improved for clarity (see below).
- **Coverage:** The assessment assumes all clusters are zonal; regional clusters should also be considered using `--region` instead of `--zone` where appropriate.
- **Fail Criteria:** Correctly identifies that `false` or unset is a fail.
- **Alternative API:** Direct use of the GKE REST API (`projects.locations.clusters.get`) is possible, but `gcloud` is sufficient for most audit automation.

---

### 3. IMPROVEMENTS

- **Cluster Enumeration:** Explicitly document the need to enumerate both zonal and regional clusters. Example:
  ```
  gcloud container clusters list --format="value(name,location,locationType)"
  ```
  Then, use `--zone` or `--region` as appropriate.
- **API Option:** For large environments, consider using the GKE API directly or Google Cloud Asset Inventory for bulk queries.
- **Program Name Suggestion:** For maximum clarity and alignment with naming conventions, consider:
  - `gcp_gke_cluster_control_plane_authorized_networks_enabled`
  This makes it clear that the check is for the control plane, not node pools.
- **Edge Cases:** Note that if `masterAuthorizedNetworksConfig` is present but has an empty list of CIDRs, it may technically be "enabled" but not actually restricting access. Consider checking that the list of authorized networks is non-empty for full security.
- **Documentation:** Recommend including a note about the need to check all projects, not just a single project.

---

### 4. CONFIDENCE: **HIGH**

- The assessment is technically accurate and aligns with GCP best practices and API capabilities.

---

### 5. FINAL RECOMMENDATION: **Modify**

- **Minor modifications** are recommended:
  - Explicitly document the enumeration of both zonal and regional clusters.
  - Suggest a slightly improved program name: `gcp_gke_cluster_control_plane_authorized_networks_enabled`
  - Optionally, add logic to ensure that the list of authorized networks is not empty.
  - Clarify that all projects should be audited, not just a single project.

---

**Summary:**  
The assessment is correct in its classification and technical approach. With minor improvements to enumeration and naming, this control can be robustly and automatically audited using GCP APIs and the `gcloud` CLI. No manual review is required unless there are edge cases not covered by the automation.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identify that the status of Control Plane Authorized Networks (Master Authorized Networks) for GKE clusters is directly exposed via the GCP API and can be queried using the Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud`). The property `masterAuthorizedNetworksConfig.enabled` is readily available for all clusters, making this control fully automatable without manual intervention. Enumeration of all clusters and checking this property is straightforward and supported by GCP tooling.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
gcp_gke_cluster_master_authorized_networks_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **GCP Resource/Service:** Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) Clusters
- **API/CLI Method:**  
  - List clusters: `gcloud container clusters list --format="value(name,zone)"`
  - Describe cluster: `gcloud container clusters describe $CLUSTER_NAME --zone $COMPUTE_ZONE --format="get(masterAuthorizedNetworksConfig.enabled)"`
- **Properties to Check:**  
  - `masterAuthorizedNetworksConfig.enabled`
- **Pass Criteria:**  
  - The property `masterAuthorizedNetworksConfig.enabled` is set to `true` for every GKE cluster.
- **Fail Criteria:**  
  - The property is `false` or not set for any GKE cluster.

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
There were no substantive disagreements between the AI opinions; both agreed on automation and the technical approach. The final decision incorporates the completeness suggestion from the second opinion regarding cluster enumeration.
